title: MARTHA'S HOUSE - 211info
text: MARTHA'S HOUSE MARTHA'S HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transitional housing to families and women escaping domestic violence or experiencing homelessness. Also offers supportive services including social service referrals, job readiness, budgeting, and other life skills in preparation for independent living. Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-18, or single women. Must be alcohol and drug free, and no pets. Hours: House: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Office: Tuesday-Thursday 9am-noon and 1pm-3pm, Friday 9am-noon Email (541) 571-3963 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 571-3963 Main phone Main phone (541) 571-3963 Martha's House Director & House Manager Stephen Boulton Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website or call for more information or to begin application process. FEES: Deposit for key, drug test fee, and program fees may be charged DOCUMENTS: Completed application, photo ID LANGUAGES: English, Spanish interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Cities of Boardman, Echo, Hermiston, Irrigon, Stanfield, and Umatilla Services: Transitional Housing/Shelter * Homeless Families Transitional Housing/Shelter * Homeless Women If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. No Mapping Information Available
